fields.description:
## Why

The overlay mechanism (the EXISTING `if:` skip-guard + a deterministic trigger-precompute step) and
the genuinely-new `batch` step need an end-to-end proof that they compose and run, independent of the
plan-review migration. A small disposable workflow is the cheapest proof and doubles as the
visual-editing refinement sample.

## What

Author a throwaway demo workflow under examples/ that exercises BOTH: at least one criterion (prompt)
step conditionally included via `if: ${{ steps.<triggers>.outputs.<name> }}` (driven by a deterministic
precompute step — the overlay mechanism) AND a `batch` step over a small prompt group, runnable
end-to-end under the FakeAgentRunner (offline, no live calls). Add a dry-run test asserting the
constructs behave (the if:-gated step is included/skipped per the trigger; the batch-plan is journaled;
no live calls). Label it clearly DISPOSABLE — epic B removes it after the migration, retaining it
meanwhile as the visual-editing refinement sample.

## Scope

- src/rebar/llm/workflow/examples/<demo>.yaml (clearly labeled throwaway).
- A test (tests/unit/workflow/) running it under FakeAgentRunner and asserting if:-gated inclusion +
  batch-plan journaling, with zero live calls.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A demo workflow under examples/ uses BOTH an `if:`-gated overlay step (with a precompute trigger)
      and the `batch` step.
- [ ] A test runs it end-to-end under FakeAgentRunner (offline) and asserts the if:-gated step is
      included/skipped per its trigger and the batch-plan is journaled; no live LLM calls occur.
- [ ] The workflow is clearly labeled disposable (removed by epic B; meanwhile the visual-editing sample).
- [ ] make lint + the workflow test suite pass.
